content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

December 20, 1976

Mr. Crane Kitchel
Acting County Counsel
1507 West Fifth Street
San Bernardino, California 92415

Dear Mr. Kitchel:

Thank you for your letter of November 9, 1976, in which
you inquire whether a joint powers agency is required to
develop a Conflict of Interest Code pursuant to §87300 of
the Government Code.

It is our view that joint powers agencies formed pursuant
to Government Code §6500 et seq. are "public agencies" by vir-
tue of their makeup, which generally consists of a number of
local agencies joined for the purpose of carrying out mutual
governmental functions.

Government Code §87300 provides in pertinent part that
"Every agency shall adopt and promulgate a Conflict of Interest
Code...". (Emphasis added.) Therefore, joint powers agencies,
as combinations of local agencies, should be required to develop
a Conflict of Interest Code. Further, local public officials
who sit on these joint powers agencies may have different dis-
closure requirements under a joint powers code than as members
of City Councils. This is because the official's reporting
requirements under a Conflict of Interest Code and Government
Code §87200 are tied to the official's jurisdiction, which
generally will not be the same in both instances.

To date the Commission has not yet established deadline
dates for joint powers agencies for which the Commission will
be the code reviewing body. If and when the Commission and
appropriate code reviewing bodies do establish deadline dates,
the agencies affected will no doubt receive sufficient notice.

Government Code §82011 will determine the appropriate code
reviewing body for all agencies as follows:

"Code reviewing body" means:

(a) The Commission, with respect to the
Conflict of Interest Code of ... any local
government agency with jurisdiction in more
than one county;

Crane Kitchel
December 20, 1976                                Page Two


     (b) The Board of Supervisors, with respect
to the Conflict of Interest Code of any county
agency other than the Board of Supervisors, and
of any local government agency, other than a
city agency, with jurisdiction wholly within
the county;

     (c) The city council, with respect to the
Conflict of Interest Code of any city agency
other than the city council; ....


If you have any further questions, please call me at
916/322-6441.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Alfredo Flores
                                        Research Specialist
                                        Conflicts of Interest
                                        Division

AF:gb

-                                                                                                          /

OFFICE OF THE                          p p
COUNTY COUNSEL

County Civic Building
551 West Fifth Street
San Bernardino, CA 92415
(714) 383-2701

M. CRANE KITCHEL
Acting County Counsel

                                    COUNTY OF
                        8 57 AM '16 SAN BERNARDINO

Roger N. Kehew, Jr.
Chief Deputy County Counsel
Eugene L Hidley
Chief Deputy County Counsel
E. H Robinson, Jr.
Deputy County Counsel
William Sabourin
Deputy County Counsel
Robert R Walker
Deputy County Counsel
Paul A. Godley
Deputy County Counsel
Richard Wm. Strong
Deputy County Counsel
Charles A. Duerbeck
Deputy County Counsel
Craig S. Jordan
Deputy County Counsel
Alan K. Marks
Deputy County Counsel
Leonard D. Britney
Deputy County Counsel
Dean N. Zabriskie
Deputy County Counsel
Clark H Alsop
Deputy County Counsel
Dawn Stafford
Research Attorney

SAN BERNARDINO, CALIFORNIA

November 9, 1976



Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814

RE: OPINION REQUEST

Gentlemen:

We request your opinion on the following question:

Is a joint powers entity, which is created by an agreement pursuant
to the provisions of Article I, Chapter 5, Division 7, Title 1
of the Government Code (§6500 et seq.), an agency required to
adopt a conflict of interest code pursuant to provisions of the
Political Reform Act?

In this connection, we note that Government Code Section 87300
provides in its pertinent part that "Every agency shall adopt
and promulgate a Conflict of Interest Code . . ." (emphasis added),
and that Government Code Section 82041 provides as follows:

"Local government agency" means a county, city or district
of any kind including school district, or any other local
or regional political subdivision, or any department,
division, bureau, office, board, commission or other
agency of these, but does not include any court or
any agency in the judicial branch of government.(emphasis added)

The definition of entity found in Section 6542 of the Government
Code provides that:

"Entity" as used in this article means any agency,
board or commission provided for by a joint powers
agreement pursuant to Article 1 of this chapter.
Such agency, board or commission is an entity separate
from the public agencies which are parties to such
agreement.

Fair Political Practices Commission
November 9, 1976
Page 2

In light of the definition of entity, it would appear that a
joint powers entity may not be an agency required to adopt a
code pursuant to Government Code Section 87300.

Respectfully submitted,

M. CRANE KITCHEL
Acting County Counsel

MCK:DS:kgh

sections.question: Is a joint powers entity, which is created by an agreement pursuant to the provisions of Article I, Chapter 5, Division 7, Title 1 of the Government Code (§6500 et seq.), an agency required to adopt a conflict of interest code pursuant to provisions of the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion: It is our view that joint powers agencies formed pursuant to Government Code §6500 et seq. are "public agencies" by virtue of their makeup, which generally consists of a number of local agencies joined for the purpose of carrying out mutual governmental functions. Government Code §87300 provides in pertinent part that "Every agency shall adopt and promulgate a Conflict of Interest Code ... ". Therefore, joint powers agencies, as combinations of local agencies, should be required to adopt a Conflict of Interest Code. Further, local public officials who sit on these joint powers agencies may have different disclosure requirements under a joint powers Code than as members of City Councils.
sections.question_synthetic: The County Counsel of San Bernardino inquires whether joint powers agencies formed under Government Code §6500 et seq. are required to develop and adopt a Conflict of Interest Code under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes that joint powers agencies are "public agencies" and therefore must adopt a Conflict of Interest Code under Government Code §87300. Officials serving on these agencies will have different disclosure requirements under a joint powers code than their respective local agency roles.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request letter and FPPC response. Response authored by Alfredo Flores, Research Specialist. No formal "CONCLUSION" section header but conclusion is clearly delineated from opening statement. Deadline dates not yet established at time of letter.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Is a joint powers entity, which is created by an agreement pursuant to the provisions of Article I, Chapter 5, Division 7, Title 1 of the Government Code (§6500 et seq.), an agency required to adopt a conflict of interest code pursuant to provisions of the Political Reform Act?

CONCLUSION: It is our view that joint powers agencies formed pursuant to Government Code §6500 et seq. are "public agencies" by virtue of their makeup, which generally consists of a number of local agencies joined for the purpose of carrying out mutual governmental functions. Government Code §87300 provides in pertinent part that "Every agency shall adopt and promulgate a Conflict of Interest Code ... ". Therefore, joint powers agencies, as combinations of local agencies, should be required to adopt a Conflict of Interest Code. Further, local public officials who sit on these joint powers agencies may have different disclosure requirements under a joint powers Code than as members of City Councils.

embedding.summary: FPPC advises San Bernardino County Counsel that joint powers agencies formed under Government Code §6500 et seq. are required to develop and adopt Conflict of Interest Codes, and identifies the appropriate code reviewing bodies based on jurisdictional scope.
